query: Do the mapped fluvial systems indicate the presence of paleolakes associated with the inverted channels?

row.rationale: Fluvial sedimentology — direct Earth-analog sedimentary geology
row.paper: Davis, J.M., et al., 2019. A diverse array of fluvial depositional systems in Arabia Terra. JGR Planets, 124(7), pp.1913-1934. DOI:10.1029/2019JE005976
row.query: Do the mapped fluvial systems indicate the presence of paleolakes associated with the inverted channels?

tool_input.summary: No dedicated PDS catalog datasets explicitly labeled as “inverted channels”, “fluvial system maps”, or “paleolake mapping” were returned by the catalog searches. To evaluate whether mapped fluvial systems imply paleolakes associated with inverted channels, the most directly useful PDS holdings found here are (1) global MOLA topography products for basin/closed-contour analysis and (2) MRO CTX image products for morphologic context of inverted channels and potential shoreline/deltaic features.

candidates.title: MOLA Topography Data in IMG Format
candidates.reasoning: Use these gridded/IMG-format MOLA topography products to test whether inverted-channel reaches terminate in local topographic lows and whether there are closed basins/possible paleolake basins coincident with mapped fluvial systems.

candidates.title: MOLA Topography Images in IMG and PNG Format
candidates.reasoning: Quicklook/topography image products useful for rapid screening of candidate basins (potential paleolakes) near inverted-channel networks before doing quantitative basin/shoreline analysis.

candidates.title: VOLUME MROX_0001: MRO CTX EDR ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: CTX images provide regional-scale morphology needed to see whether inverted channels feed into depositional basins (e.g., fan/delta-like terminations, possible shoreline benches) that would support a paleolake interpretation.

candidates.title: ATM Volume mgs_0001 (Mars Global Surveyor Sampler CD)
candidates.reasoning: A PDS3 MOLA PEDR sampler volume appeared in search results; while only a sampler, it indicates availability of MOLA profile/experiment data useful for spot-checking elevations across suspected paleolake basins associated with inverted channels.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
Do the mapped fluvial systems indicate the presence of paleolakes associated with the inverted channels?

parts.args: {"query": "Mars inverted channels paleolake mapping", "mission": "Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter", "limit": 20, "fields": "summary"}
{"query": "Mars THEMIS daytime IR global mosaic fluvial", "mission": "Mars Odyssey", "instrument": "THEMIS", "limit": 20, "fields": "summary"}

datasets.title: MOLA Topography Data in IMG Format
instruments: MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER for MGS

datasets.title: MOLA Topography Images in IMG and PNG Format
instruments: MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER for MGS

parts.args: {"query": "HiRISE RDR", "mission": "Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter", "instrument": "HIGH RESOLUTION IMAGING SCIENCE EXPERIMENT", "limit": 10, "fields": "summary"}
{"query": "THEMIS IR RDR", "mission": "Mars Odyssey", "instrument": "THERMAL EMISSION IMAGING SYSTEM", "limit": 10, "fields": "summary"}
{"query": "HRSC Mars image", "mission": "Mars Express", "instrument": "HIGH RESOLUTION STEREO CAMERA", "limit": 10, "fields": "summary"}

parts.args: {"summary":"No dedicated PDS catalog datasets explicitly labeled as “inverted channels”, “fluvial system maps”, or “paleolake mapping” were returned by the catalog searches. To evaluate whether mapped fluvial systems imply paleolakes associated with inverted channels, the most directly useful PDS holdings found here are (1) global MOLA topography products for basin/closed-contour analysis and (2) MRO CTX image products for morphologic context of inverted channels and potential shoreline/deltaic features.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mex_marsis_optim:topography_data::1.0","title":"MOLA Topography Data in IMG Format","mission":"MARS GLOBAL SURVEYOR","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Use these gridded/IMG-format MOLA topography products to test whether inverted-channel reaches terminate in local topographic lows and whether there are closed basins/possible paleolake basins coincident with mapped fluvial systems."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mex_marsis_optim:topography_image::1.0","title":"MOLA Topography Images in IMG and PNG Format","mission":"MARS GLOBAL SURVEYOR","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Quicklook/topography image products useful for rapid screening of candidate basins (potential paleolakes) near inverted-channel networks before doing quantitative basin/shoreline analysis."},{"dataset_id":"MRO-M-CTX-2-EDR-L0-V1.0","title":"VOLUME MROX_0001: MRO CTX EDR ARCHIVE","mission":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"img","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"CTX images provide regional-scale morphology needed to see whether inverted channels feed into depositional basins (e.g., fan/delta-like terminations, possible shoreline benches) that would support a paleolake interpretation."},{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-MOLA-3-PEDR-L1A-V1.0","title":"ATM Volume mgs_0001 (Mars Global Surveyor Sampler CD)","mission":"Mars Global Surveyor","node":"atm","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"A PDS3 MOLA PEDR sampler volume appeared in search results; while only a sampler, it indicates availability of MOLA profile/experiment data useful for spot-checking elevations across suspected paleolake basins associated with inverted channels."}]}
